path.description: path to the node as legs from the slide root: the first leg is always nodes, then an integer node index; nested container legs are left/right (two_column), cells (grid), or body (card). NOTE: list items and prose paragraphs are node fields, NOT legs — to change them, replace the whole list/prose node with edit_slide_node. examples: [nodes, 2] or [nodes, 0, left, 1]

node.description: the inserted slide node as a JSON object whose kind discriminator is one of: hero|heading|prose|list|callout|quote|chip|table|two_column|grid|card|card_section|flow|chart|code_block|image|divider|arrow|section_divider|decoration. Every RichText field is an ARRAY of FLAT runs — [{"text":"hi"}] or [{"text":"38% lower","bold":true}]; there is NO nested style object and keys are lowercase. The variant key is NOT named kind: list uses listKind (bullet|ordered|checklist) + items[].text; callout uses calloutKind (info|tip|warning|success|error) + title + body; flow uses steps[] of {label:RichText, detail:RichText, icon?} — NOT title/body. Examples: list {"kind":"list","listKind":"bullet","items":[{"text":[{"text":"first"}]}]}; callout {"kind":"callout","calloutKind":"tip","title":"Heads up","body":[{"text":"detail"}]}; flow {"kind":"flow","steps":[{"label":[{"text":"Start"}],"detail":[{"text":"kick off"}]}]}. Call describe_node for the full per-kind shape.
